description:
The **AWSSupport-ValidateMWAADependencies** runbook validates Amazon Managed Workflows for Apache Airflow (MWAA) environment Python dependencies by executing the [amazon-mwaa-docker-images](https://github.com/aws/amazon-mwaa-docker-images/) test command on an EC2 instance. It also packages wheel files for environments without public network access.  

### Use cases:

1. **Dependency Validation**: Verify that dependencies (requirements file) with default or custom constraints are compatible before upgrading or downgrading the MWAA environment to a specific Airflow version.
2. **Wheel File Packaging**: Generate packaged wheel files in `plugins.zip` for environments that lack public network access.

### Notes:

1. Additional AWS costs will apply during execution. The automation provisions a `t3.large` EC2 instance with a 50 GB `gp3` volume in your account via CloudFormation. This instance fetches the `amazon-mwaa-docker-images` repository, validates dependencies, packages wheel files and is automatically terminated after execution.
2. If unsupported Python modules are detected: For versioned modules, the specific version is updated or removed, and the test command reruns. For unversioned modules, the module is commented out, and the test command reruns. This process continues until all modules are supported or the maximum retry limit (**20** attempts) is reached.
3. Wheel file packaging executes only when the requirements file contains a `--find-links` directive pointing to a plugin path and the `--no-index` flag.
4. Requirements file and custom constraints file must be stored in the S3 bucket specified in the input parameters. And all generated files including the updated `requirements.txt` file, `plugins.zip` file (if applicable), logs and the report are uploaded to the same S3 bucket.
5. This runbook only supports Airflow version >= 2.9.2. If validation for previous version is required please use [aws-mwaa-local-runner](https://github.com/aws/aws-mwaa-local-runner).

S3BucketName.description: (Required) The name of the Amazon S3 bucket in your account where you want to upload the troubleshooting logs. Make sure the bucket policy does not grant unnecessary read/write permissions to parties that do not need access to the collected logs.

RequirementsFilePath.description: (Required) The S3 object key of requirements file in the S3 bucket.

ConstraintsFilePath.description: (Optional) The S3 object key of custom constraints file in the S3 bucket.

TargetAirflowVersion.description: (Required) The Airflow version to validate the dependencies. Target version must be supported by MWAA.

SubnetId.description: (Required) The subnet that has public network access to launch the MWAA runner EC2 instance.

AutomationAssumeRole.description: (Optional) The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) role that allows Systems Manager Automation to perform the actions on your behalf. If no role is specified, Systems Manager Automation uses the permissions of the user that starts this runbook.

AcknowledgeIAMPolicyWarnings.description: (Required) This runbook pre-checks whether your IAM role has the required permissions using SimulatePrincipalPolicy. This check may produce inaccurate results if your policy uses Conditions or resource-level restrictions. Select YES to acknowledge this and proceed.

mainSteps.name: GetBucketPublicStatus
mainSteps.description: Checks if the target Amazon S3 bucket potentially grants **read** or **write** `public` access to its objects.

mainSteps.name: ValidateInput
mainSteps.description: Validates the input parameters including IAM permissions, S3 bucket location and ownership, file existence, target Airflow version, and subnet public network access.

mainSteps.name: BranchOnValidationResult
mainSteps.description: Branches the workflow based on whether the input parameters are valid.
Get the latest AMI for Amazon Linux 2023 image.
mainSteps.name: GetLatestAMI

mainSteps.description: Create a CloudFormation stack including an IAM role, an instance profile, a security group and an MWAA runner EC2 instance.
mainSteps.name: CreateMWAARunnerStack

mainSteps.description: Get the EC2 instance ID from the CloudFormation stack output.
mainSteps.name: GetInstanceId

mainSteps.description: Check the EC2 instance status and wait for the initialization.
mainSteps.name: WaitForInstanceInitialization

mainSteps.description: Run commands on the instance to validate the MWAA dependencies and download wheels files if required.
mainSteps.name: RunCommands

commands: # Copyright Amazon.com, Inc. or its affiliates. All Rights Reserved.
# Licensed under the Amazon Software License  http://aws.amazon.com/asl/
# Purpose: Validate and fix Python requirements for MWAA environment
# Shared state (set by functions, used across sections)
# Logging helper: prints to terminal AND appends to FULL_LOG
# Error context helper: prints last 20 lines of FULL_LOG to terminal on error
# This allows users to see error details in the step output without needing
# to download the full log from S3.
echo "WARNING - Below are the last 20 lines of the log for"
echo "troubleshooting. The full log will be uploaded to S3 if the"
# Wait for user data initialization to complete before proceeding
log "Waiting for user data initialization to complete..."
log "⚠ cloud-init reported errors during user data execution, continuing anyway"
cd ~ || { log "✗ ERROR: Failed to change to the home directory. Please verify the environment and try again."; print_error_context; exit 1; }
# Install docker-compose with pinned version and SHA256 verification
log "✗ ERROR: Failed to download docker-compose. Please verify your network connectivity and try again."
log "✗ ERROR: Failed to download docker-compose checksums. Please verify your network connectivity and try again."
log "✗ ERROR: SHA256 checksum verification failed for docker-compose."
# Clone the MWAA Docker images repository
log "⚠ First clone attempt failed, retrying in ${retry_delay}s..."
log "✗ ERROR: Failed to clone the MWAA Docker images repository. Please verify your network connectivity and try again."
# Extract Python version and constraint link from the cloned repo

log "✗ ERROR: The requirements.txt file was not found for Airflow version ${AIRFLOW_VERSION} in the MWAA Docker images repository. Please verify that the target Airflow version is correct and supported by amazon-mwaa-docker-images (https://github.com/aws/amazon-mwaa-docker-images/tree/main/images/airflow)."
log "✗ ERROR: No constraint line was found in the requirements.txt for Airflow ${AIRFLOW_VERSION}. Please verify that the target Airflow version is correct and supported by MWAA."
log "✗ ERROR: Failed to extract the constraint URL or Python version from the requirements.txt. Please verify that the requirements file is in a valid format and try again."
log "✗ ERROR: Failed to install Python ${PYTHON_VERSION}. Please verify your network connectivity and try again."
cd amazon-mwaa-docker-images/ || { log "✗ ERROR: The cloned repository directory was not found. Please try running the automation again."; print_error_context; exit 1; }
log "Creating Python ${PYTHON_VERSION} virtual environment for Airflow ${AIRFLOW_VERSION}..."
log "✗ ERROR: Failed to create the virtual environment for Airflow ${AIRFLOW_VERSION}. Please check the full log for details and try again, or contact AWS Support for assistance."

cd "images/airflow/${AIRFLOW_VERSION}/" || { log "✗ ERROR: The Airflow ${AIRFLOW_VERSION} directory was not found in the repository. Please verify that the target Airflow version is correct and supported by MWAA."; print_error_context; exit 1; }
# SECTION 2: Download and Validate Requirements File
if ! aws s3api get-object --bucket "${S3_BUCKET}" --key "{{ RequirementsFilePath }}" --expected-bucket-owner "${BUCKET_OWNER}" ./requirements/requirements.txt >> "$FULL_LOG" 2>&1; then
log "✗ ERROR: Failed to download the requirements file from S3. Please verify that the file path is correct and the IAM role has s3:GetObject permission on the bucket."
log "✗ ERROR: The requirements file is empty or contains only comments. Please provide a valid requirements file with at least one dependency and run the automation again."
# Check if plugins path exists (for later wheel download)
log "✓ Plugins path detected in requirements"
log "Updating default constraint to target version..."
# Extract path whether quoted (--constraint "/path") or unquoted (--constraint /path)

log "✗ ERROR: A custom constraints path was detected in the requirements file, but the ConstraintsFilePath parameter was not provided. Please re-run the automation with a valid ConstraintsFilePath parameter."
log "Downloading constraints from s3://${S3_BUCKET}/{{ ConstraintsFilePath }} to ./requirements/constraints.txt"
if aws s3api get-object --bucket "${S3_BUCKET}" --key "{{ ConstraintsFilePath }}" --expected-bucket-owner "${BUCKET_OWNER}" ./requirements/constraints.txt >> "$FULL_LOG" 2>&1; then
log "✓ Custom constraint file downloaded and configured"
log "✗ ERROR: Failed to download the custom constraints file from S3. Please verify that the file path is correct and the IAM role has s3:GetObject permission on the bucket."
# Download constraints file for later module version lookups
log "Downloading constraints file for version lookups..."
log "✓ Constraints file copied from local custom constraints"
log "⚠ Failed to download constraints file from ${CONSTRAINT_URL}"
# Pattern 5: "Invalid requirement", signal error to caller
# Strip any carriage returns from the requirements file (handles Windows line endings)
# Special handling for apache-airflow: pin it to the target AIRFLOW_VERSION
# Check if already pinned to the target AIRFLOW_VERSION
log "✗ ERROR: apache-airflow is already pinned to ${AIRFLOW_VERSION} but still failing. This is an unexpected error."
# Pin apache-airflow to the target AIRFLOW_VERSION
log "ERROR: apache-airflow identified as failed module but not found in requirements.txt"
# Find the line number of this module (case-insensitive, must be at start of line)
log "ERROR: Module '${module}' not matched. File contents:"
# Check if the line has a version specifier
# Look up the module version in the constraints file
# Check if the current version already matches the constraints file
# Pin to the version from constraints file
# Module not found in constraints file — remove version specifier
echo "${module} (version removed, not in constraints, consider adding to custom constraints)" >> "$CHANGED_MODULES"
# No version specifier — comment it out
./run.sh test-requirements >> "$FULL_LOG" 2>&1  # First run - warm up environment
./run.sh test-requirements > "$VALIDATION_OUTPUT" 2>&1  # Second run - capture for parsing
log "✗ ERROR: Validation did not complete. Check the full log for details."
log "✓ All requirements validated successfully after ${iteration} iteration(s)"
log "✗ ERROR: Invalid requirement detected. Please review the error log for details and ensure the requirements file is in valid format."
log "✗ ERROR: Could not identify the failed module from the validation output. Please review the error log for details and resolve the dependency issue manually."
log "✗ ERROR: The failed module '${failed_module}' was not found in the requirements file. Please review the error log for details and resolve the dependency issue manually."
log "✗ ERROR: Validation did not complete. Check the full log for details."
log "⚠ Reached maximum iterations (${MAX_ITERATIONS}), some issues may remain. Please rerun the automation using the updated requirements file."
log "⚠ Some wheel files may have failed to download"
cd "$plugins_dir" || { log "✗ ERROR: Failed to access the plugins directory. Please try running the automation again."; print_error_context; exit 1; }
zip -q "$plugins_zip" -- * || { log "✗ ERROR: Failed to create the plugins zip archive. Please try running the automation again."; print_error_context; exit 1; }
cd "$work_dir" || { log "✗ ERROR: Failed to return to the working directory. Please try running the automation again."; print_error_context; exit 1; }
log "✗ ERROR: Failed to upload plugins.zip to S3. Please verify that the IAM role has s3:PutObject permission on the bucket and try again."
log "⚠ No wheel files downloaded, skipping upload"
# Upload requirements file (may have updated constraints or modified dependencies)
log "✗ ERROR: Failed to upload the requirements.txt to S3. Please verify that the IAM role has s3:PutObject permission on the bucket and try again."
log "✗ ERROR: Failed to upload the error log to S3. Please verify that the IAM role has s3:PutObject permission on the bucket and try again."
log "✗ ERROR: Failed to upload the full log to S3. Please verify that the IAM role has s3:PutObject permission on the bucket and try again."

mainSteps.description: Cleanup the created resources by deleting the CloudFormation stack.
mainSteps.name: Cleanup
GenerateReport
mainSteps.description: Generates the final report based on previous steps' output and uploads the report to the Amazon S3 bucket.
